methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Recursion Pharmaceuticals

data.narrative:
Alto Neuroscience, a public-stage biotech & health company founded by Amit Etkin, carries a current valuation of $529M against $253M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 3 public-stage biotech & health companies: Tempus AI ($6.0B), Freenome ($2.6B), and Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The median valuation across these three same-sector, same-stage comparables stands at $2.6B, with a range spanning $1.8B to $6.0B. Public-comp anchoring using price-to-sales multiples is not applicable; while the biotech & health sector median P/S is 5.8x across three public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), Alto's revenue figures are not available on file, precluding multiplication-based valuation.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $2.2B (low) to $3.0B (high), centered at $2.6B median. This implies material upside from Alto's current $529M mark. Key uncertainties include the wide $1.8B–$6.0B peer range, which reflects heterogeneous business models and market positions within the comparable set, and the absence of revenue or profitability data to weight peers or apply alternative methodologies. A more granular peer selection or disclosure of Alto's financial metrics would narrow this band.
